summary: Implemented the deterministic M6 Alpha start-to-victory simulation loop through authoritative sim-core state, scheduler-driven GameCommand submission, events, query, save/load, and Node/Worker-compatible replay. The loop reuses M2 economy/routes, M3 governance/succession, M4 campaign/postwar, M5 playable-loop evidence, and M6 diplomacy/legitimacy/policy-event substrate, and records explicit hash-covered terminal states for victory, defeat, and continued-play.

tests_run.result: On feat/m6-start-to-victory-sim-loop; changes are limited to allowed task paths, with new M6 loop test and worker files untracked before review.
Passed format:check, lint, full workspace typecheck, and architecture:check.
Passed 25 test files / 191 tests for existing sim-core suite.
Passed 1 test file / 4 tests covering victory, defeat, continued-play, malformed command rejection, save/load mid-run, no invariant corruption, and Node/Worker hash parity.
Passed 1 test file / 10 tests, including M6 Alpha terminal command/query schema parsing and malformed terminal id rejection.
Passed 2 test files / 16 tests for save-format and save/load contracts.
Passed 2 test files / 3 tests; existing AI tests remain on shared command path.
Passed; M6 Alpha Node hash 260f46c3 matched Worker-compatible hash 260f46c3, loaded hash 260f46c3, terminal outcome victory.
Passed architecture:check, sim:determinism, root vitest 39 files / 296 tests, and apps/web vitest 1 file / 4 tests.

decisions: Added protocol-level versioned M6 Alpha terminal outcome command/query and script fixture so player and AI can use the same GameCommand path without UI or out-of-band mutation.

Stored M6 Alpha terminal states in WorldState runtime as canonicalized, validated, hash-covered state; terminal query can derive a read-only assessment before commit and returns committed terminal state after command submission.

The M6 Alpha fixture starts from accepted M4/M5 evidence and adds only the minimal M3 succession, M6 diplomacy/legitimacy, and policy-event runtime needed to prove one deterministic Alpha victory path.
Save-format V1 now round-trips the M6 Alpha runtime slice so mid-run save/load and replay preserve terminal-state determinism.
risks: R4 task touches protocol, save-format, world-state hashing, and command/query dispatcher; systems architecture review should verify compatibility posture before QA route.
M6 Alpha scenario fixture is intentionally minimal and not M7 full content; it proves deterministic substrate integration rather than final campaign balance.
Terminal outcome thresholds are deterministic Alpha acceptance thresholds, not final design tuning.
requested_action: Review the M6 Alpha start-to-victory loop and R4 protocol/save/state changes, then route to qa_reviewer if architecture review passes. Do not mark ACCEPTED/CLOSED from this handoff alone.
